course_sections.section_function: Explains how a source-backed mechanism produces a measurable output.

knowledge_cards.module_title: Generic mechanism as a source-backed causal module
knowledge_cards.core_exam_claim: A source-backed mechanism links input, process, output, and limitation.
key_definitions: A mechanism is a source-backed causal explanation from input to output.
knowledge_cards.exam_ready_knowledge_synthesis: The examinable point is the causal link between mechanism and output, with the limitation stated explicitly.
knowledge_cards.mechanism_or_process_logic: Input changes the mechanism; the mechanism changes the readout; the limitation defines scope.
knowledge_cards.canonical_example: A source-backed example demonstrates the same causal chain without adding unsupported detail.
knowledge_cards.exam_use: Use this module to answer a short-answer or case prompt asking why the output changes.
knowledge_cards.common_error_or_trap: Do not state the output without the causal process.

official_definition_records.definition_text: A source-backed causal explanation from input to output.

limitations: Past papers control emphasis only, not factual truth or exact future wording.

content_coverage_checks.message: Every visible card has source support, star priority, exam specificity, protected coverage, and coverage status.
